nameMeanings.name: Iddon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from the Middle English female personal name Ideyn, Idon, Idony (Old French Idone, Latin Idonea ‘suitable’). See also Ideson .

variants.name: Iden
meanings: English: habitational name from Iden Green in Benenden (Kent), recorded as Ydennec. 1200, or Iden Manor in Staplehurst (Kent) recorded as Idenne in 1313. The placenames derive from Old English īw ‘yew tree’ + denn ‘pasture’.
English: in Lancashire, a variant of Iddon, from the Middle English female personal name Ideyn, Idon, Idony (Old French Idone, Latin Idonea ‘suitable’).
North German: metronymic or patronymic from the personal name   Ide   2.

variants.name: Iddins
meanings: English: variant of Iddon with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Ideson , Ison .

variants.name: Ideson
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Eddison  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes that William Edeson also appears as Ideson, 1583–6 in the Rothwell Parish Registers, and that in Skipton Parish Registers Humphrey Edson or Eedson, whose son Humphrey was baptized in 1695, was the ancestor of a succession of Humphreys, whose surname appears as Ideson from 1724. The name is correctly pronounced /ɪdəsən/.  Compare  Iddison  .

2 English: (i) relationship name from the Middle English female personal name Ide + son. It may also have been used as a pet form of Idon(y), see (ii). (ii) relationship name, alternatively perhaps from the Middle English female personal name Idon(y), Ideyn ( see Iddon ) + son, with reduction of Idonson or Ideynson to Ideson. However, it is uncertain if either name in (i) and (ii) gave rise to a hereditary surname.

nameMeanings.name: Llywarch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional, now only occasionally used: compound of the god's name Lugu- ( see Lleu ) + Old Celtic marcos ‘horse’.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Llywarch ap Iddon was born in Llanrhyddlad, Gwynedd, Wales, United Kingdom as the son of Iddon ap Seth. He had at least 1 daughter.
